6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Over the past few years, I’ve bought more online courses than I’d like to admit. Python, System Design, AWS, AI, databases, cloud architecture, you name it, I’ve probably enrolled in a course for it. Eventually, though, I noticed something interesting. I wasn’t struggling to find courses anymore. I was struggling to find courses that matched what I actually wanted to learn.
That realization led me down a different path. Instead of comparing online courses, I started comparing course creation platforms. At first, I assumed they were all designed for instructors who wanted to sell educational content. Then I discovered a new generation of AI-powered platforms that weren’t just helping creators build courses, they were helping learners generate personalized courses for themselves.
Over several weeks, I tested ten of the most popular course creation platforms. Some focused on helping businesses sell digital products, while others used AI to build customized learning experiences. They solved very different problems, but by the end of my comparison, one platform stood out because it completely changed how I think about online learning.
If you’re looking for the best course creation platform in 2026, these are the five I’d recommend.
What Makes a Great Course Creation Platform?
One thing I realized early in this comparison is that not every course creation platform has the same goal.
Traditional platforms are designed for creators. They help instructors upload videos, organize lessons, manage payments, and sell courses to thousands of students. AI-powered platforms, on the other hand, are increasingly focused on the learner. Instead of asking you to choose from an existing catalog, they generate a learning experience around your own goals.
That difference matters because most professionals don’t need another generic beginner course. They need focused learning that matches their existing knowledge. The best platforms understand that personalization is becoming just as important as content management.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

1. Fenzo.ai — Best Overall Course Creation Platform
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Fenzo.ai was easily the biggest surprise of my comparison.
When I first opened the platform, I expected another AI chatbot with a few educational features. Instead, I found something that felt much closer to an intelligent course designer. Rather than browsing through a marketplace of existing courses, I could describe exactly what I wanted to learn, how much experience I already had, and how deeply I wanted to explore a topic. Within minutes, it generated a structured learning path that felt like it had been designed specifically for me.
What impressed me most wasn’t just the initial course generation. As I progressed, I could ask follow-up questions, request simpler explanations, generate quizzes, dive deeper into difficult topics, or skip concepts I already understood. The learning experience remained interactive instead of static, which made it feel far more personal than traditional online courses.
If your goal is learning rather than selling courses, this is the most interesting course creation platform I’ve used.
2. Kajabi — Best for Building an Online Business
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Kajabi has earned its reputation as one of the most complete platforms for creators, and after spending time with it, I understand why.
It goes far beyond course hosting by including email marketing, sales funnels, landing pages, communities, memberships, and payment processing in one place. If your primary goal is turning educational content into a business, Kajabi offers one of the strongest all-in-one solutions available.
It’s clearly built for entrepreneurs rather than learners, but within that space it does an excellent job of reducing the number of tools you need to manage.
3. Thinkific — Best for Professional Educators
Thinkific strikes a nice balance between simplicity and flexibility.
The course builder is easy to navigate, lesson organization feels intuitive, and there are enough customization options to create polished learning experiences without becoming overwhelming. I particularly liked how straightforward it was to structure modules, quizzes, assignments, and downloadable resources.
For instructors who want a reliable course creation platform without needing advanced marketing features, Thinkific remains one of the strongest choices.
4. Teachable — Best for Simplicity
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Not every creator wants dozens of customization options.
Sometimes you simply want to record your lessons, organize them into modules, publish the course, and start teaching.
That’s exactly where Teachable performs well.
The interface stays out of your way, the publishing workflow is straightforward, and it’s one of the easiest platforms to learn if you’re creating your first online course. It doesn’t offer the same depth as some competitors, but that simplicity is precisely what many creators are looking for.
5. LearnWorlds — Best Interactive Learning Experience
LearnWorlds focuses heavily on learner engagement, and that immediately sets it apart from many traditional course platforms.
Interactive videos, assessments, certificates, and built-in engagement tools make the experience feel much more dynamic than simply watching recorded lectures. If you’re creating courses for students who benefit from regular interaction, LearnWorlds provides plenty of tools to keep the learning experience engaging.
It’s particularly appealing for organizations, training providers, and educators who want to move beyond passive video lessons.
Which Course Creation Platform Should You Choose?
The answer depends entirely on what you’re trying to accomplish.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

If you’re a creator building an education business, the traditional platforms still make a lot of sense. But if you’re primarily trying to learn new skills, I think AI-powered platforms represent a much more exciting direction.
Instead of adapting yourself to someone else’s curriculum, you’re creating a learning path that already understands where you’re starting.
Who I Think Will Benefit Most
One thing this comparison reinforced is that different users need different kinds of course creation platforms.
If you’re an educator, coach, or entrepreneur, your priorities are likely things like payments, branding, marketing, and student management. Traditional platforms have spent years refining those workflows, and they’re excellent at what they do.
If you’re a software engineer, student, AI practitioner, or lifelong learner, your priorities are different. You’re not trying to build a course business, you simply want the fastest path to understanding a new topic. That’s exactly where Fenzo.ai stood out for me because it removes so much of the friction between curiosity and learning.
My Biggest Takeaway
The biggest lesson from this comparison wasn’t that AI can build courses faster.
It was realizing that most online learning has been designed around the needs of instructors rather than the needs of learners.
Traditional courses have to appeal to thousands of people with different backgrounds, which naturally makes them broad. An AI-powered course creation platform doesn’t have that limitation. It can build a curriculum around your existing knowledge, your goals, and even the pace at which you want to learn.
That feels much closer to how experienced professionals actually develop new skills throughout their careers.
Final Thoughts
If you’d asked me a year ago what the best course creation platform looked like, I probably would have pointed you toward one of the established names like Kajabi or Thinkific. Those platforms continue to do an excellent job helping creators build and sell educational content.
After spending weeks comparing today’s options, though, I think the conversation is beginning to change.
Platforms like Fenzo.ai are redefining what a course can be. Instead of publishing one curriculum for everyone, they allow every learner to have a curriculum built specifically for them. I don’t think traditional platforms are going away anytime soon, but I do think personalized, AI-generated learning is where the industry is heading.
If your goal is teaching thousands of students, I’d recommend one of the established creator platforms. If your goal is to become a faster, more efficient learner, Fenzo.ai is the course creation platform I’d choose today.
